name: Cold Glazed Salmon
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg yolk, hard cooked and mashed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, combine the white wine, basil leaves, 3 tarragon stems, shallots, rosemary sprigs, lemon slices, and celery leaves. Simmer for 20 minutes or until reduced to about 3 tbsp.
2. Lay the salmon on heavy foil twice as long as the fish. Fold up the edges of the foil and pour the wine mixture over the fish. Season with salt and fold the foil tightly, crimping the edges to secure them.
3. Place the wrapped salmon in a preheated 375°F oven for 50-60 minutes or until it flakes easily with a fork.
4. Carefully open the foil, remove the skin from the top of the fish just below the head to the tail, and discard any brown flesh. Set aside the remaining foil and skin.
5. Peel and slice the turnip into thin pieces. Place in ice water to cool.
6. Mash the egg yolk with butter until smooth. Reserve at room temperature.
7. Brush a thin layer of chilled fish aspic over the salmon, then arrange the tarragon stems and turnip slices decoratively on top.
8. Brush the remaining aspic over the entire surface. Using a pastry bag fitted with a decorative tip, pipe small amounts into the centers of the turnip flowers.
9. Chill the salmon, covered, for at least 2 hours or up to 6 hours before serving.
